title: We Know Thee Who Thou Art

T: We Know Thee Who Thou Art
%%combinevoices 1
T: (also known as Prayer After Pardon)
C: Words: William Bright, 1865. 
C: Music: 'Sellinge' John Pyke Hullah, 1868. Setting: "Hymns Ancient and Modern", 1904.

w: 1.~We know Thee who Thou art, Lord Je- sus, Ma- ry's Son; 
w: 2.~That sac- red fount of grace, 'Mid all the bliss of Heav'n, 
w: 3.~Brought home from ways per- verse, At peace Thine arms with- in,

w: 5.~We know Thee who Thou art, Our own re- deem- ing Lord;

w: We know the yearn- ings of Thy heart To end Thy work be- gun. 
w: Has joy when- e'er we seek Thy face, And kneel to be for- giv'n.

w: But oh! when next they tempt us, give More strength to cling to Thee. 
w: Be Thou by will, and mind, and heart, Ac- cep- ted, loved, a- dored.

c_lines: Music: 'Sellinge' John Pyke Hullah, 1868. Setting: "Hymns Ancient and Modern", 1904.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2013 Revision.

1.raw_text: We know Thee who Thou art, Lord Jesus, Mary's Son; We know the yearnings of Thy heart To end Thy work begun.

2.raw_text: That sacred fount of grace, 'Mid all the bliss of Heav'n, Has joy whene'er we seek Thy face, And kneel to be forgiv'n.

3.raw_text: Brought home from ways perverse, At peace Thine arms within, We pray Thee, shield us from the curse Of falling back to sin.

4.raw_text: We dare not ask to live Henceforth from trials free; But oh! when next they tempt us, give More strength to cling to Thee.

5.raw_text: We know Thee who Thou art, Our own redeeming Lord; Be Thou by will, and mind, and heart, Accepted, loved, adored.
